surfaces.description: Registry, DiagramKind, plugin registration, CLI capability metadata, and mutation-op projection.
Shared family detection and agent parse route the family consistently.
Modeled subset and source-preservation policy are explicit in code/docs.
Modeled and opaque/segment-preserved syntax round-trip without silent loss.
A public narrower and mutation ops are exposed; opaque fallback bodies stay source-level only.
Verification catches render-blocking conditions with named warning/error codes.
No ambient nondeterminism; repeated renders/layouts are stable or controlled by explicit options.
SVG/PNG-capable rendering path is covered.
ASCII/Unicode rendering path is covered.
layoutMermaid/verify layout expose truthful geometry for the family.
Region metadata for editor/TUI/accessibility is covered or tracked.
Live editor supported examples and glyphs cover the family.
Human docs, agent docs, SDK declarations, llms.txt, and skills are synchronized.
Shared skill benchmark includes a fixture-backed case tagged for the family.
Official upstream Mermaid examples/tests are harvested into an executable docs corpus, family bench, or accounted parser/DB bench.
Known compatibility divergences in harvested upstream examples/tests are executable and documented.
Pinned Mermaid syntax inventory is accounted for with executable native semantics or a named, tested safety divergence; parse-only preservation is not parity.
Family-specific semantic/layout invariants are tested beyond render smoke.
Mermaid and a Wikipedia/domain reference define a recognizable family hallmark that is protected by geometry assertions and reviewer-visible artifacts.
Goldens, snapshots, or reviewer evidence cover representative output.
Generated site samples/gallery surfaces cover the family.
Package files/exports/consumer init artifacts expose the family surface.
Targeted mutation/sabotage lane exists or is tracked.

fidelity.signature: Directed node-link topology with semantically distinct shapes and exposed arrowheads.
States and transitions with pseudostates, composite frames, notes, and concurrency regions.
Participants above lifelines with chronological messages, activations, blocks, creation, and destruction.
Ordered chronology on a visible rail with periods, sections, and event cards.
UML compartment boxes, namespaces, cardinalities, and distinct relationship endpoint symbols.
Entity and attribute boxes joined by distinguishable crow-foot cardinalities with group containment.
Ordered scored tasks grouped into sections with actor ownership and a satisfaction trajectory.
Services, groups, junctions, icons, and side-aware links arranged as a bounded system map.
Measured Cartesian axes with bars or lines, series identity, legends, and point labels.
Radial part-to-whole slices or donut with proportional angles, labels, legend, and emphasis.
Two labeled axes divide four named regions containing normalized points and collision-free leaders.
Time-scaled task bars grouped by section with statuses, milestones, exclusions, and dependencies.
A central idea with deterministic bilateral hierarchy, curved branches, authored shapes, and local pictograms.

gitgraph.label: GitGraph
gitgraph.role: branching commit history and release topology

fidelity.signature: Source-ordered commits on distinct branch lanes with merges, cherry-picks, tags, and commit marks.
